steps.title: Recognize the logical puzzle
steps.content: The passage sets up an apparent paradox: thirteenth-century artifacts are found at a settlement founded in the fourteenth century. If both facts are true, what must logically also be true to make them compatible?

steps.title: Determine the logical requirement
steps.content:
For thirteenth-century artifacts to exist at a fourteenth-century-founded settlement, those artifacts cannot have originated at the site—they must have arrived after its founding through trade, migration, or similar means.

This is exactly what the correct answer states. The other options don't resolve the core incompatibility:
  • A discusses how common artifacts from different periods are
  • C speculates about where the founders came from without addressing the artifact timeline
  • D mentions excavation damage to unrelated materials
.

That confirms B is correct.
